outputChannelCompensations.generatedFile: ClawCode fires PostToolUse for tool calls but the in-payload schema for file-write tools beyond Bash has not been enumerated against the source; F19 routes file-write paths through require-review until per-tool wiring is added.
outputChannelCompensations.commitMessage: ClawCode commit-text surfaces flow through PostToolUse on git-invoking tools; F19 routes commit-message writes through require-review (compensating stricter rule).
outputChannelCompensations.prText: ClawCode PR-text surfaces flow through PostToolUse on gh-CLI-style tools; F19 routes prText writes through require-review (compensating stricter rule).
outputChannelCompensations.finalMessage: ClawCode does not expose a pre-emit hook for the assistant's final message; F19 routes finalMessage writes through require-review (compensating stricter rule).

negativeCapabilities.reason: ClawCode does not include cwd in the hook stdin payload — the working directory is passed to the subprocess via cwd=working_directory but the JSON context fields are session_id, tool_call_id, tool_name, tool_input only
negativeCapabilities.evidence: clawcode/llm/agent.py:1318-1323 (PreToolUse fire) and clawcode/plugin/hooks.py:259 (cwd passed to subprocess, not to payload)

negativeCapabilities.reason: ClawCode hook surface does not expose execution-time env baselines; F15 envelope build/verify cannot be wired
negativeCapabilities.evidence: envelopeReporting: false; no env-related fields in the verified PreToolUse / PostToolUse payloads

negativeCapabilities.reason: Whether ClawCode routes MCP tool invocations through the same PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook surface has not been verified end-to-end. The hook matcher accepts any tool_name regex, so operator configuration determines coverage; mark unverified until live MCP tool fire is observed
negativeCapabilities.evidence: clawcode/plugin/hooks.py:86-93 (matcher_supported_events includes PreToolUse for any tool); no explicit MCP-tool dispatcher verified

negativeCapabilities.reason: Whether ClawCode surfaces Skill invocations through the PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook surface has not been verified end-to-end; same matcher-config dependency as MCP
negativeCapabilities.evidence: clawcode/plugin/hooks.py:86-93; ClawCode's claw_skills/ surface exists but hook fire path is not yet traced

negativeCapabilities.reason: ClawCode is a Claude-Code-compatible fork (clawcode/plugin/hooks.py:69) and inherits the no-pre-emit-hook limitation; final-message output is neither observed nor intercepted
negativeCapabilities.evidence: outputChannels.finalMessage = none; no pre-emit final-message hook discovered in the ClawCode hook events enumeration

negativeCapabilities.reason: PostToolUse + toolOutput are covered (tool_output field in clawcode/llm/agent.py:1428-1434); generatedFiles / commitMessage / prText flow through PostToolUse only when their tool names match the operator's matcher; finalMessage and screenshots have no surface

F15 execution-envelope build/verify requires harness-side env baseline capture which ClawCode does not expose. Decision lattice still re-checks critical writes against contract-declared envelopes; this is the harness limitation, not an ARG gap
negativeCapabilities.evidence: envelopeReporting: false; no exec-time envelope surface in clawcode/plugin/hooks.py

compensatingRestrictions: Decision-engine sources ClawCode IR trustMeta as: argsFidelity=exact, cwdFidelity=opaque, envelopeReporting=false. Floors that need cwd MUST NOT trust the adapter's extractCwd output (it falls back through unused payload paths); branch context is discovered server-side from git, not from the payload.

Operators wiring ClawCode are expected to configure the PreToolUse matcher regex to cover every shell-class tool name their ClawCode build emits (default: "Bash"). MCP and Skill interception status depends on the matcher regex.

The adapter emits a ClawCode-native JSON decision on stdout via harnessOutput="permission-json" because ClawCode parses stdout JSON for the permission decision and IGNORES the exit code (clawcode/plugin/hooks.py:38-51 + 252-280). The adapter also still exits 2 on block for cross-harness consistency.
